reasons: Esmeralda is a strong Diggie counter because Diggie's ultimate gives shields, and Esmeralda can benefit from enemy shield interactions.
This makes Diggie's defensive value less clean in teamfights.

proof.summary: Diggie protects allies with shields and control immunity, while Esmeralda can punish shield-heavy formations by converting enemy shields into value for herself.
worksBestWhen: Esmeralda can reach shielded heroes after Time Journey.
Esmeralda is not kited before she can convert shield value.
failureCases: Esmeralda cannot enter the fight after Diggie uses Time Journey.

reasons: Hilda can be listed as a conditional map-control counter into Diggie.
Hilda can check bushes, trigger or absorb Diggie bomb pressure, then recover using bush sustain instead of giving up map control.

proof.summary: Diggie wants to control bushes and objective entrances with bombs, while Hilda can safely check map areas, absorb bomb pressure better than fragile heroes, and recover through bush sustain.
worksBestWhen: Hilda is played as an aggressive roamer who actively checks bushes and river entrances.
Hilda turns bomb-checking into real map control or pressure.
failureCases: Diggie's team chains damage after Hilda triggers bombs.
Anti-heal or burst prevents Hilda from resetting in bushes.
Hilda only eats bombs without creating vision or pressure.

reasons: Natalia can pressure Diggie before a fight starts and force him to use resources defensively.
This remains useful because Diggie is strongest when he can safely hold Time Journey for the enemy's main engage.

proof.summary: Diggie wants safe positioning behind allies, while Natalia can threaten him from camouflage and make him play more defensively.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks before Diggie groups with teammates.
failureCases: Diggie's team keeps reveal, traps, or grouped positioning.
Natalia is revealed before reaching Diggie.

reasons: Kaja can isolate Diggie before Diggie uses Time Journey for the real fight.
Suppression can force Diggie into a defensive timing instead of letting him protect his team.

proof.summary: Diggie's value depends on saving Time Journey for the correct engage window, while Kaja can force him into a pre-fight pickoff or defensive ultimate.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches Diggie before the main teamfight.
Kaja has teammate burst ready after the drag.
failureCases: Diggie saves ultimate for the real engage despite Kaja's pressure.

reasons: Franco can punish exposed Diggie with hook or suppression before Diggie's ultimate timing becomes valuable.
This is especially useful around bushes and objectives.

proof.summary: Diggie wants safe proximity to allies, while Franco punishes exposed support positioning with hook pressure and suppression.
worksBestWhen: Franco controls bush vision before objectives.
Franco threatens Diggie before Time Journey becomes valuable.
failureCases: Diggie plays behind minions or tanks.
Franco misses hook and cannot reach suppression range.
